content:
## Mar 2, 2026 — Daily Opportunity Scan

### 1. 🔥 NMBR | Loblaw $2.4B Expansion = 9,700 New Jobs + 70 Stores (HIGH VALUE)
- **WHAT:** Loblaw just announced $2.4B investment in 2026 — 70 new stores, 191 renovations, ~9,700 new jobs across Canada. Biggest Canadian retail expansion this year.
- **WHY IT MATTERS:** Massive payroll demand. Loblaw's tech vendors (POS, scheduling, workforce management) serving these stores will need embedded payroll for thousands of new hires. Any vertical SaaS platform in retail/restaurant/pharmacy space serving Loblaw franchisees is a hot Nmbr target.
- **SUGGESTED ACTION:** Identify which workforce management or retail SaaS platforms Loblaw and its franchisees use. Those platforms are potential Nmbr partners who just got a 9,700-employee tailwind.

### 2. 📊 DIVIDENDS | Great-West Lifeco 10% Dividend Increase
- **WHAT:** Great-West Lifeco (GWO) announced a 10% bump to quarterly dividend → $0.67/share, payable end of March. Long track record of steady increases.
- **WHY IT MATTERS:** Canadian financial sector showing strength. GWO is a boring, reliable dividend grower — right in Drew's wheelhouse. Also: CT REIT (CRT.UN) paying monthly at 5.5% yield, and Hamilton Champions Enhanced CDN Dividend ETF (HCAL) paying $0.06/month.
- **SUGGESTED ACTION:** If looking to add a financial sector dividend grower, GWO at the new rate is worth a look. Not advice — just flagging the signal.

### 3. ⚖️ CHARLIE | PI Law Firms: Cost Per Lead Hit $442 on Google Ads
- **WHAT:** New 2026 benchmark data shows personal injury firms now paying $442/lead on Google Ads vs $183/lead on SEO. Industry article calls AI chat qualification "eliminating the black hole where leads go to die."
- **WHY IT MATTERS:** At $442/lead, PI firms are bleeding money if those leads aren't contacted instantly. Charlie's speed-to-contact value prop is directly validated by this data. The industry is explicitly saying AI qualification is the 2026 priority.
- **SUGGESTED ACTION:** Use this $442 stat in Charlie pitches — "You're paying $442 per lead and letting them sit for hours? Charlie responds in seconds." That's a concrete ROI story.

### 4. 🏢 NMBR | ADP Canada Pushing "2026 Workplace Trends" — Narrative Opportunity
- **WHAT:** ADP Canada published their 2026 trends report emphasizing AI deployment, multi-generational workforce management, and new regulations. Getting wide coverage in HR media.
- **WHY IT MATTERS:** ADP is shaping the narrative around complexity. Every time ADP says "Canadian payroll is getting more complex," that's a Nmbr talking point — SaaS platforms shouldn't try to build this themselves.
- **SUGGESTED ACTION:** Reference ADP's own complexity messaging in partner conversations. "Even ADP says this is getting harder — why would you build it when you can embed Nmbr?"

### 5. 📰 NMBR | Vertical SaaS Specialization Named Top Engineering Trend for 2026
- **WHAT:** CIO magazine and multiple SaaS publications are calling vertical SaaS specialization one of the defining trends of 2026 — platforms going deep in one industry rather than broad.
- **WHY IT MATTERS:** This is literally Nmbr's market. More vertical SaaS platforms = more potential partners who need embedded payroll. The trend validates the whole thesis.
- **SUGGESTED ACTION:** No immediate action — but good ammo for pitch decks and partner conversations. The market is moving toward Nmbr, not away from it.

**Still open from previous scans:**
- Platform Expand partnership outreach (Feb 28) — still unreached
- CT REIT monthly dividend at 5.5% — still paying

---

## Mar 1, 2026 — Daily Opportunity Scan

No new high-value signals today. Weekend news cycle was quiet. Yesterday's top opportunities remain actionable:
- **Platform Expand partnership** — still the #1 action item (unreached)
- **Enbridge dividend** — 3% increase to $0.97/quarter now paying at new rate as of today. ~5.5% yield. Also flagging CT REIT (CRT.UN) as a monthly dividend payer at 5.5%.
- No new Charlie competitive threats or payroll industry moves.

---

## Feb 28, 2026 — Daily Opportunity Scan

### 1. 🔥 NMBR | Platform Expand — New Canadian Market Entry Service (HIGH VALUE)
- **WHAT:** Platform Expand (subsidiary of Sparkcorpnet) just launched 3 premium services helping international companies incorporate and expand into Canada — packages from $1,990. Announced Feb 25.
- **WHY IT MATTERS:** Every company they help set up in Canada will need Canadian payroll. Platform Expand is a potential channel partner or referral source for Nmbr — they're literally creating Nmbr's ideal customers.
- **SUGGESTED ACTION:** Reach out to Platform Expand about a payroll referral partnership. Their clients are international companies entering Canada who have zero payroll infrastructure — perfect Nmbr fit.

### 2. 📊 NMBR | Tech Hiring Boom in Canada — 48% of IT Managers Expanding Teams
- **WHAT:** Robert Half data shows 48% of Canadian IT hiring managers plan to expand teams in 2026, with demand spreading beyond Toronto/Vancouver/Montreal to secondary cities.
- **WHY IT MATTERS:** More hiring = more payroll processing = more demand for embedded payroll solutions. SaaS companies serving these growing tech employers need payroll capabilities.
- **SUGGESTED ACTION:** Use this stat in partner sales conversations — "your customers are scaling headcount, they need payroll now" is a compelling narrative for vertical SaaS partners.

### 3. 🏢 NMBR | Toronto Downtown Office Comeback — Wealthsimple, Lyft, Nvidia Expanding
- **WHAT:** Major tech companies (Wealthsimple, Lyft, Nvidia) are taking significant office space in downtown Toronto, fueling a commercial real estate comeback per Royal LePage.
- **WHY IT MATTERS:** These are large employers scaling Canadian operations. Their HR/workforce management SaaS vendors will need embedded payroll. Also signals that Canada-expansion trend is accelerating.
- **SUGGESTED ACTION:** Monitor which HR/workforce SaaS platforms these companies use — those platforms are potential Nmbr partners.

### 4. ⚖️ CHARLIE | Law Firms Investing Heavily in AI Automation
- **WHAT:** Multiple industry reports this week confirm law firms are accelerating AI adoption for client intake, billing efficiency, and lead management in 2026.
- **WHY IT MATTERS:** PI law firms are Charlie's sweet spot. The narrative is shifting from "should we use AI?" to "which AI do we use?" — Charlie needs to be in that conversation.
- **SUGGESTED ACTION:** Content play — position Charlie case studies around speed-to-contact stats for PI firms. The 6x stat is the killer differentiator.

### 5. 📈 MARKET | ADP Canada Publishing 2026 Workplace Trends
- **WHAT:** ADP Canada released their 2026 workplace trends report — focused on multi-generational teams, AI deployment, and new regulations.
- **WHY IT MATTERS:** Competitive intel. Know what ADP is telling the market so Nmbr can counter-position. ADP selling complexity = Nmbr selling simplicity through embedded solutions.
- **SUGGESTED ACTION:** Skim the ADP report for talking points to use (or counter) in partner conversations.

---
